role_title: Program Manager (Homeland Layered Defense), Public Sector

level_signals: Lead and supervise a team of 3-4 delivery managers
Base salary range $232,800–$291,000 consistent with senior principal-level

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years professional experience, ideally managing complex technical programs for DoD or national security customers
must_haves.qualifier: ideally managing complex technical programs for DoD or national security customers

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: A proven ability to lead large, cross-functional efforts across delivery, contracts, and customer teams
A healthy obsession with establishing and driving adoption of programmatic systems and processes; plus the ability to exert influence and manage associated change
Familiarity with FAR-based contracts and program structures like IDIQs, task orders, and OTAs
Strong business acumen with the ability to manage performance metrics, contract deliverables, and program budgets
A track record of high ownership in ambiguous environments
Excellent written and verbal communication skills — especially with senior government stakeholders
Deep curiosity about agentic AI and its application to homeland layered defense
A willingness to travel up to 25% of the time

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Proficiency in Python, SQL or other programming languages
A proven track record in B2B + B2G client facing roles and expanding client relationships
Prior experience delivering technical solutions to government customers
Domain expertise in a relevant field (e.g. modeling and simulation, joint planning processes, intelligence workflows, etc)

scope.team_size_estimate: 3-4 direct reports (delivery managers) plus oversight of technical delivery team
scope.surface: Scale's portfolio of homeland layered defense clients; integrating data pipelines for computer vision and GenAI software deployments
scope.business_model: B2G (government contracts, DoD/national security), prime contractor model with subcontractors
primary_metrics_inferred: Customer satisfaction with senior government stakeholders
core_responsibilities: Serve as the contractual and internal program lead across a large-scale public sector engagement, integrating multiple sub-efforts under one umbrella
Own relationships with senior government stakeholders and ensure that we meet contractual obligations, performance metrics, and reporting requirements
Own relationships with subcontractors to ensure successful delivery of the prime contract
Lead formal engagements such as contract reviews, customer program syncs, performance assessments, and reporting cycles
Lead and supervise a team of 3-4 delivery managers who run day-to-day customer engagement efforts
Oversee a technical delivery team that will drive product roadmap alignment that adapts evolving customer needs

compensation.equity_form: equity grant subject to Board of Director approval

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($232,800–$291,000 base)
Equity mentioned as part of compensation package
Clear scope of responsibility and team size stated
Mission-driven role with concrete national security context
Comprehensive benefits including health, dental, vision, retirement, learning stipend
red_flags: Active TS/SCI clearance is a hard blocker — non-transferable requirement with long lead times to obtain
US citizenship almost certainly required (TS/SCI prerequisite) though not explicitly stated in JD text
Role appears DC-area onsite; remote eligibility not addressed
90-day waiting period policy for reconsidering candidates for the same role

fields_inferred_not_stated: location.type (inferred as onsite from Washington DC salary band and no remote language)
location.work_auth (inferred as us-citizenship-required from TS/SCI clearance requirement — US citizenship is a prerequisite for TS/SCI)
location.visa_sponsorship (inferred as no from TS/SCI requirement)
seniority.level (inferred from comp range + 10+ years + people management signals)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities, not directly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from B2G context and prime contractor framing)
ambiguities: Work authorization is not explicitly stated in the JD, but TS/SCI clearance requires US citizenship; treated as us-citizenship-required
Office/remote policy is not explicitly stated; salary is tied to Washington DC location which implies onsite or at minimum DC-area presence
The 'nice to haves' section uses the language 'many have' rather than 'preferred' or 'nice to have' — treated as soft/nice-to-have given the non-mandatory framing
